findings.message: test_slo_request_latency_default_is_reasonable uses a wide range assertion (100–5000) that is strictly weaker than the exact assertion in test_defaults (slo_request_latency_p99_ms == 200.0). The new test adds no safety the existing test does not already provide and could mask a regression if the default drifts to an extreme-but-in-range value.
findings.suggestion: Remove this test or replace the range with an exact equality assertion matching test_defaults. If the intent is a separate guard on 'reasonableness', at minimum tighten the bounds (e.g. 150–300) so the test fails on a surprising drift.

findings.message: test_resolved_log_level_production_is_not_debug calls Settings(environment='production') but the canonical production environment string in this codebase is 'prod' (used in test_resolved_log_level_is_info_outside_dev and test_explicit_log_level_overrides_resolution). 'production' is an unrecognised/non-canonical value; because resolved_log_level returns INFO for any string that is not 'dev', the assertion passes trivially on any unknown string. The test provides false confidence: it does not cover the actual production environment.
findings.suggestion: Change environment='production' to environment='prod' to match the canonical value used throughout the codebase and in the parametrised existing test. Optionally also assert the return value equals 'INFO' rather than != 'DEBUG' so the test fails if a new unexpected level is introduced.
